topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an unnerving intensity, casting harsh shadows across the cubicles. Liam smoothed down his tie for the tenth time, his palms growing slick against the crisp fabric. He stared at the printed application, the words blurring slightly at the edges. Beside him, Maya was humming softly, tapping a pen against her resume with a steady rhythm. Every time Liam glanced over, he felt a tightening in his chest, a sudden, cold clench that made his breath catch. He took a shallow, quick breath, forcing his gaze back to the blank space on the form, hoping the ink wouldn't suddenly smear. The silence between them felt vast, pregnant with unspoken competition.

[story 2]

"Are you sure about this, Alex?" Sarah whispered, her voice thin as tracing paper. She fiddled incessantly with the strap of her messenger bag, her fingers tracing the worn leather repeatedly. Alex felt a tremor run through his hands; they were shaking just enough to make holding the pen a clumsy endeavor. He looked at the identical job description on the screen, the requirement list seeming to multiply in his vision. A knot formed low in his stomach, a heavy, unpleasant weight that made his shoulders hunch forward. He swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the effort painful, and finally managed to sign his name, the motion feeling slow and agonizingly deliberate.

[story 3]

I kept re-reading the interview questions, the script of my own potential future, and a wave of nausea washed over me. The air in the waiting room felt thick, suffocating. My heart was hammering against my ribs, a frantic, uneven drumbeat I couldn't quiet. Across the small table, Ben was laughing easily, a bright, open sound that grated on my nerves. I focused intently on the pattern of the carpet, trying to anchor myself to something solid, anything, as my entire frame felt rigid, poised on the edge of sudden collapse. When it was my turn, my voice came out unexpectedly high, thin, and reedy.
